title: sarcostemma cynanchoides var. hartwegii
intro: Sarcostemma cynanchoides var. hartwegii, also known as the 'Bitter Apple', is a succulent plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal benefits.
